poor: Your room is fighting the music harder than you'd like. Let's fix that.
The room is doing more harm than good right now — but these things are fixable.
Acoustically, this is a tough environment. The data tells us exactly where to start.
This room needs work before it can deliver what your gear is capable of.
okay: Solid starting point — a few targeted changes will unlock a lot here.
Decent fundamentals. The low-hanging fruit is clear from the data.
Your room is in the ballpark. Some refinement and it'll really open up.
Not bad at all. You're closer to great than you might think.
good: Good room. The foundations are there; it's about fine-tuning now.
Strong result. Your setup is doing the right things — the details are the next frontier.
This is a room that rewards careful listening. Most of the work is already done.
Good performance. The remaining issues are small and well-targeted.
excellent: Excellent result. Your room is working with the sound, not against it.
This is a well-optimised space. Serious work has gone into this setup.
Outstanding. You're in the top tier for domestic listening environments.
Exceptional. The room is nearly transparent — the music is what you're hearing.

poor: Significant peaks and dips — bass modes are dominating the response.
The room is adding and removing frequencies before they reach your ears.
Strong modal activity below Schroeder — bass is uneven at the listening position.
You're hearing the room more than the recording in the low end.
okay: Some peaks and dips present. Treatment or placement can smooth these.
Modal behaviour is present but manageable. Speaker position is worth experimenting with.
The response isn't flat, but nothing here is catastrophic. Targeted treatment helps.
A few lumps in the bass region. Placement changes often resolve these before reaching for treatment.
good: Reasonably flat response. Minor modal issues remain.
Good frequency balance. The remaining irregularities are subtle.
The response is smooth for a real room. Any remaining peaks are well within tolerance.
Solid performance. Minor treatment at first-reflection points could tighten this further.
excellent: Very flat response. Excellent modal control.
Impressively even through the bass and midrange. The room is behaving.
Near-reference flatness. Whatever you're doing with placement or treatment, it's working.
Textbook response. The modal region is tightly controlled.

poor: Strong early reflections are blurring the image and muddying transients.
Reflections are arriving too early and too loud — the stereo image will suffer.
The room is contributing a lot of reflected energy. Side and ceiling absorption is a priority.
First reflections are competing with the direct sound. Imaging and detail are taking a hit.
okay: Reflections are present but manageable. Side and ceiling absorption helps.
Moderate reflections. A panel at the first-reflection points would make a clear difference.
Some reflected energy arriving at the listening position. Not critical, but improvable.
Reflections are audible in the data. Diffusion or absorption at the key points will tighten things up.
good: Reflections are well controlled. Imaging should be solid.
Good reflection management. The direct sound is clearly dominant.
First reflections are at a reasonable level. Stereo imaging should be stable and precise.
Solid reflection control. The room isn't adding much confusion to the soundstage.
excellent: Excellent reflection control. The direct sound dominates cleanly.
Reflections are minimal. The stereo image will be sharp and well-defined.
Impressive early-reflection management. The room gets out of the way.
Near-anechoic performance in the early window. Outstanding result.

poor: Low-frequency support is weak. The room isn't reinforcing bass effectively.
Sub-bass is missing or inconsistent — small room acoustics are limiting the low end.
The usable bandwidth is narrow. You may be missing significant low-frequency content.
Bass extension is limited. A subwoofer or larger room would give you the foundation you're missing.
okay: Bandwidth is limited in places. Subwoofer or room treatment may help.
Reasonable extension, but the low end rolls off earlier than ideal.
The room delivers acceptable bandwidth. Some low-end treatment could extend the usable range.
Mid-range bandwidth is solid; the extreme lows need attention.
good: Good bandwidth. The room delivers solid low-frequency support.
Strong extension into the low frequencies. The room is working in your favour.
Broad usable bandwidth. You're hearing most of what the recording contains.
Good low-end reach. The room is supporting the bass without artificially boosting it.
excellent: Full, extended bandwidth. Excellent low-end foundation.
Exceptional bandwidth. You're accessing the full frequency range of the recording.
Deep extension with even distribution. The room isn't limiting you anywhere.
Outstanding low-frequency reach. Reference-level performance in terms of bandwidth.

poor: Left/right tonal balance is off. Check speaker symmetry and room layout.
Significant asymmetry in the stereo image. The room or placement is pulling the centre.
The listening position is compromised by an asymmetric room response.
Tonal balance is uneven. Symmetric speaker placement relative to room boundaries is the first fix.
okay: Minor balance asymmetry. Offset listening position may be the cause.
Slight left/right imbalance in the tonal response. Usually addressable with position tweaks.
Some tonal asymmetry is present. Room symmetry, not just speaker placement, matters here.
Balance is close but not perfect. Try centering the listening seat relative to the room width.
good: Good left/right balance. The stereo field should be centred and stable.
Solid tonal symmetry. The stereo image should sit correctly in the room.
Good balance. The room is treating both channels consistently.
Tonal balance is well-maintained. Stereo imaging should feel natural and stable.
excellent: Near-perfect stereo balance. The room is symmetrical where it counts.
Excellent tonal symmetry between channels. The image will be locked and precise.
Outstanding balance. This is a genuinely symmetrical acoustic environment.
Both channels are delivering the same tonal signature. The stereo image has a solid foundation.

poor: Highly uneven response — strong comb filtering or flutter echo present.
Spectral roughness is high. Parallel surfaces are creating serious reflective interference.
The response is jagged across multiple frequency bands. Flutter echo is likely a factor.
Severe colouration from room reflections. Diffusion and absorption are both needed.
okay: Some spectral roughness. Diffusion or additional absorption will help.
Moderate unevenness in the mid-range. Diffusive surfaces can break up the interference patterns.
Comb filtering is present but not dominant. Panel placement and diffusers can address this.
The response has some texture to it. Livening up the room with diffusion often helps more than adding more absorption.
good: Smooth response overall. No serious colouration issues detected.
Good spectral smoothness. The room isn't adding pronounced colouration.
Solid smoothness. The response texture is within normal bounds for a real listening environment.
The room response is relatively even. Fine-tuning placement can close the remaining gap.
excellent: Exceptionally smooth response. The room adds very little colouration.
Outstanding spectral smoothness. This is reference-level room behaviour.
Near-anechoic smoothness in the mid/high range. Impressive.
The frequency response is exceptionally clean. Colouration from the room is minimal.

poor: Clarity is poor — late reflections are masking transient detail.
The room's decay is too long. Reverberant energy is smearing transients.
Instruments blur into each other. The room is holding on to sound for too long.
RT60 is high for a listening environment. Acoustic treatment will bring the decay under control.
okay: Clarity is average. Tightening the reflection control will help definition.
Transients are somewhat smeared. Reducing the reverb tail would improve musical clarity.
Decay time is longer than ideal. Bass traps and absorptive panels at reflection points will help.
Reasonable clarity, but the room adds a noticeable tail to transient events.
good: Good clarity. Transients come through cleanly.
Solid transient definition. The room's decay is well-controlled.
Good clarity performance. You're hearing most of the fine detail in the recordings.
The room resolves transients well. Timing cues in the music should feel accurate.
excellent: Excellent clarity. The room preserves fine transient detail beautifully.
Outstanding transient resolution. The room is fast and transparent.
Exceptional clarity. The decay is controlled enough to reveal fine musical detail.
Near-studio-level clarity. Transient events are reproduced with precision.
